chapter: Chapter: Pressure, Winds, Storms and Cyclones

questions.question: How does pressure change when the area of contact decreases?
questions.answer: Pressure increases when the area of contact decreases.

questions.question: Why do high-heeled shoes exert more pressure on the ground?
questions.answer: Because the area of contact is very small.

questions.question: Why do mountaineers carry oxygen cylinders at high altitudes?
questions.answer: Because air pressure and oxygen levels are lower at high altitudes.

questions.question: How does heating of air lead to wind formation?
questions.answer: Warm air rises and creates a low-pressure area, causing cooler air to move in as wind.

questions.question: What happens to air when it gets heated?
questions.answer: Heated air expands and becomes lighter, causing it to rise.

questions.question: Why does cool air move toward areas where warm air has risen?
questions.answer: To fill the low-pressure region created by the rising warm air.

questions.question: Why are strong winds associated with storms?
questions.answer: Large pressure differences cause air to move rapidly, producing strong winds.

questions.question: What role does warm ocean water play in cyclone formation?
questions.answer: It provides heat energy and moisture needed for cyclone formation.

questions.question: Why do cyclones weaken after reaching land?
questions.answer: Because they lose the supply of warm ocean water energy.

questions.question: Why are coastal areas more affected by cyclones?
questions.answer: Cyclones form over oceans and move toward nearby coastal regions.

questions.question: How does atmospheric pressure change with altitude?
questions.answer: Atmospheric pressure decreases as altitude increases.

questions.question: Why are barometers useful in weather forecasting?
questions.answer: They help detect changes in atmospheric pressure.

questions.question: What indicates the approach of a storm in weather observations?
questions.answer: A sudden drop in atmospheric pressure.

questions.question: Why do vehicles used in sandy areas have wide tyres?
questions.answer: Wide tyres increase area and reduce pressure on the ground.

questions.question: Why does a nail have a pointed tip?
questions.answer: To produce greater pressure and penetrate materials easily.

questions.question: What causes large-scale air movement in the atmosphere?
questions.answer: Differences in atmospheric pressure.

questions.question: What is the main feature of the eye of a cyclone?
questions.answer: It is the calm center with relatively low wind speed.

questions.question: Why do meteorologists track pressure changes over oceans?
questions.answer: To predict the formation of storms and cyclones.

questions.question: Why does air move faster when pressure difference is large?
questions.answer: Greater pressure difference produces stronger winds.

questions.question: Why is understanding pressure important in weather science?
questions.answer: Pressure differences help explain wind, storms, and cyclones.

questions.question: What happens to the surrounding air when warm air rises rapidly?
questions.answer: Cooler air rushes in to replace it.

questions.question: Why do storms often bring heavy rainfall?
questions.answer: Because rising moist air condenses to form clouds and rain.

questions.question: How do pressure differences affect weather patterns?
questions.answer: They control air movement and influence wind and storm formation.

questions.question: Why do airplanes cabins need pressurization?
questions.answer: Because atmospheric pressure is very low at high altitudes.

questions.question: Why are early warnings important before cyclones?
questions.answer: They help people take safety measures and reduce damage.
